description: Community map for sharing fruit from backyard trees in your neighborhood.

title: How I Built an AI Content Pipeline for Every Writing Post
description: Using Claude Haiku for topic tags, Supabase vector embeddings for related posts, and build-time scripts to turn 18 blog posts into a connected content product

title: How I Upgraded Search to Semantic Vector Embeddings
description: From keyword matching to meaning-based search using pgvector, Supabase, and Hugging Face — and why it's the foundation for Ask Goose

title: How I Built a Personal API Server to Control Everything Remotely
description: An Express API on the Alienware that lets me trigger jobs, check system health, and pull reports — secured with API key auth, systemd, and port knocking

title: How I Built a Cron-Powered Operations Layer on a Home Linux Server
description: What started as three cron jobs grew into 100+ lines of scheduled automation — market briefings, game bots, security audits, AI job queues, and weekly reports, all orchestrated by a single crontab

title: How I Automated My Soccer Club with a Bot
description: A Python bot on a headless Linux server that manages a TrophyManager.com soccer club — scouting, bidding, lineup setting, training, and self-grading, all automated

title: How I Built a Market Briefing That Writes and Sends Itself Every Morning
description: A Python script on a headless Linux server scrapes market data, asks Claude to explain why markets moved, and delivers it to subscribers via Resend — every weekday at 8am

title: How I Built Fruit Exchange, a Neighborhood Fruit Tree Map
description: A map-based community tool where neighbors list backyard fruit trees and others can find them — styled like a 90s farming game, verified with email, and built with Claude Code

title: How I Built a Full Status Alert System
description: Website uptime, Supabase health, API monitoring, Fail2ban reports, Claude-generated changelog, and a weekly summary — all automated from a headless Alienware

title: How I Secured the Home Linux Server
description: Layered security for an always-on home machine — UFW firewall, Fail2ban brute-force protection, SSH key-only auth, port knocking for remote access, and instant login alerts

title: How I Turned an Old Laptop Into a Headless Linux Server
description: Repurposing a 2011 gaming laptop into an always-on home server running Linux Mint — closed lid, no monitor, SSH access from anywhere

title: How I Added "TL;DR by Goose" AI Summaries to Every Post
description: Using Claude Haiku to generate static summaries at publish time — and why the architecture decision matters more than the feature itself

title: How I Built a Self-Updating Search Bar Using Claude Code
description: From architecture decision to React portal — why a simple search bar required a codebase refactor, a build script, and fixing a CSS rule I didn't know existed

title: How I Automated Daily Garmin Recaps to My Inbox
description: Building a Mac automation that fetches Garmin health data at 7am, generates AI recaps with Claude CLI, and emails them — built across two sessions

title: How I Replaced Google Forms with a Custom Contact Form Using Claude Code
description: Building a Supabase-backed contact form with email notifications in 2 hours — 4x faster than my first database project

title: How I Built 50+ Dynamic Greetings Using Claude Code
description: Replacing 8 static words with time-aware messages — and learning why iteration beats perfection

title: How I Built a Universal Footer Using Claude Code
description: Designing and deploying a production-ready footer component in under 30 minutes with terminal-based AI

title: How I Built Numerator Using Claude
description: A non-developer builds a full-stack web game from concept to deployment in four sessions

title: Gemini Grades the Website Build Difficulty
description: Google’s AI evaluates how hard it actually was to build joseandgoose.com from scratch
